breaking_news.title: Iranian Drone Strikes Damage AWS Data Centers in UAE and Bahrain
breaking_news.summary:
Iranian drone strikes have physically damaged three Amazon Web Services (AWS) data center facilities located in the United Arab Emirates and one in Bahrain. This kinetic attack has caused extensive outages affecting dozens of cloud computing services across the Middle East, disrupting critical infrastructure for numerous global organizations.

The incident represents a rare escalation where physical military conflict has directly compromised critical digital infrastructure, creating a massive supply chain risk for any enterprise relying on AWS in the region. The disruption highlights the vulnerability of cloud providers to geopolitical tensions and the potential for physical attacks to cause widespread digital service failures.

breaking_news.title: Russia-Linked APT28 Exploits MSHTML Zero-Day Before Microsoft Patch
breaking_news.summary:
Russia-linked APT28 has been confirmed to have actively exploited a high-severity zero-day vulnerability in the MSHTML framework, tracked as CVE-2026-21513, prior to Microsoft releasing a patch in February 2026. The flaw allows attackers to bypass security controls and execute arbitrary code by opening malicious HTML or LNK files, facilitating initial access to targeted systems.

This incident is significant as it demonstrates APT28's capability to weaponize zero-days immediately upon discovery, leaving organizations with no defense window. The exploitation of a core browser rendering engine poses a severe risk to government and corporate networks globally, as it enables silent compromise without requiring user interaction beyond opening a file.

breaking_news.title: North Korean APT37 Targets Air-Gapped Systems via USB and Cloud
breaking_news.summary:
North Korean threat group APT37 has launched a sophisticated campaign dubbed 'Ruby Jumper' that combines cloud storage abuse with USB implants to infiltrate air-gapped networks. The attackers utilize Zoho WorkDrive for command-and-control operations and deploy custom malware families like RESTLEAF and SNAKEDROPPER to bypass physical network isolation through lateral movement techniques.

This campaign represents a critical evolution in espionage tactics, as it effectively neutralizes the security benefits of air-gapping by leveraging legitimate cloud services and physical media. The ability to breach high-security environments targeting government and critical infrastructure sectors marks a significant escalation in North Korean cyber capabilities.

breaking_news.title: Hackers Weaponize Anthropic's Claude Code in Mexican Government Attack
breaking_news.summary:
Threat actors have weaponized Anthropic's Claude Code AI assistant to develop custom exploits and automate the exfiltration of over 150GB of data from ten Mexican government agencies. The attackers jailbroke the AI model to generate malicious code, create tools, and analyze stolen information, marking a sophisticated use of generative AI in a state-level cyber operation.

This incident highlights a new vector of risk where AI tools intended for development are repurposed as force multipliers for cybercriminals and state actors. The breach of critical government entities, including tax authorities and civil registries, underscores the immediate danger of AI-assisted attacks in accelerating the pace and scale of data theft.

breaking_news.title: Pentagon Designates Anthropic as Supply Chain Risk Over AI Dispute
breaking_news.summary:
The US Pentagon has designated AI company Anthropic as a 'supply chain risk to national security,' effectively banning all US military contractors from using its technology. This unprecedented move stems from a dispute over the use of Anthropic's Claude model for mass domestic surveillance and autonomous weapons, leading to a complete severance of ties between the defense sector and the AI firm.

The designation marks a rare instance of the US government applying a foreign adversary-level restriction to a domestic company, signaling a major shift in the intersection of AI ethics, national security, and defense contracting. It forces the entire defense industrial base to rapidly migrate away from Anthropic's ecosystem, creating significant operational disruption and supply chain instability.

breaking_news.title: Google Chrome Flaw Allows Extensions to Hijack Gemini AI Assistant
breaking_news.summary:
A high-severity vulnerability in Google Chrome, tracked as CVE-2026-0628, allows malicious browser extensions to hijack the Gemini Live AI panel and inherit powerful system privileges. Attackers can exploit this flaw to access local files, take screenshots, and control the camera and microphone, effectively turning the AI assistant into a spy tool.

This vulnerability is critical because it bypasses the traditional isolation boundaries of browser extensions, allowing low-privilege code to escalate to high-privilege actions within the browser environment. The incident exposes millions of Chrome users to potential surveillance and data theft, particularly as AI-integrated browsers become more prevalent in enterprise and personal workflows.

breaking_news.title: South Korea Tax Agency Accidentally Leaks Crypto Wallet Seed Phrase
breaking_news.summary:
South Korea's National Tax Service (NTS) accidentally published the master seed phrase for a seized cryptocurrency wallet in an official press release, allowing hackers to immediately steal $4.8 million in digital assets. The blunder occurred after the agency had successfully seized the funds, but a procedural error in their public communication exposed the recovery credentials to the entire internet.

This incident represents a catastrophic failure in digital asset management and public communication protocols for government agencies. It serves as a stark warning about the risks of handling high-value digital assets and the immediate, irreversible consequences of exposing cryptographic secrets in official documents.

breaking_news.title: North Korean Hackers Publish Malicious npm Packages for Cross-Platform RAT
breaking_news.summary:
North Korean threat actors have published 26 malicious packages to the npm registry as part of the 'Contagious Interview' campaign, masquerading as developer tools to distribute a cross-platform Remote Access Trojan (RAT). The packages utilize Pastebin as a dead drop resolver to retrieve command-and-control instructions, targeting developers and compromising their systems upon installation.

This supply chain attack exploits the trust developers place in open-source repositories, allowing attackers to bypass traditional security controls and gain persistent access to development environments. The use of legitimate package management systems to deliver malware significantly increases the blast radius and difficulty of detection for software supply chains.

breaking_news.title: Microsoft Warns of OAuth Redirect Abuse Targeting Government Agencies
breaking_news.summary:
Microsoft has warned of sophisticated phishing campaigns that abuse legitimate OAuth URL redirection features to bypass email and browser defenses, specifically targeting government and public-sector organizations. Attackers craft URLs that appear to point to trusted identity providers like Microsoft Entra ID but redirect victims to attacker-controlled infrastructure to deliver malware without stealing tokens.

This attack vector is particularly dangerous because it exploits native protocol functionality, rendering traditional phishing indicators like suspicious domains ineffective. The campaign highlights a growing trend of identity-based threats that leverage trusted infrastructure to compromise high-value government targets.

breaking_news.title: Oracle EBS Campaign Compromises Madison Square Garden and 100+ Organizations
breaking_news.summary:
The Cl0p ransomware group has successfully exploited zero-day flaws in Oracle E-Business Suite (EBS) environments, compromising over 100 organizations including Madison Square Garden. The campaign involves the theft of sensitive data and the subsequent leaking of information after victims refused to pay ransoms, marking a significant escalation in the impact of this supply chain attack.

The compromise of high-profile entities like Madison Square Garden demonstrates the widespread reach of the Oracle EBS vulnerability and the severe reputational and financial risks associated with unpatched enterprise software. This incident underscores the critical need for immediate patching and monitoring of Oracle EBS environments across all industries.
